tour.s1Body: You're about to set up your smart dashboard. This quick tour walks you through the three essential building blocks — Branches, Employees, and Departments — so everything connects and the system runs smoothly.
tour.s1BodyNamed: Great to have you, {{name}}! This quick tour walks you through the three essential building blocks — Branches, Employees, and Departments — so everything connects and the system runs smoothly.
tour.branchPageBody: We've navigated to the Branches page. This is where you manage all your physical locations. Every employee and department must belong to a branch, so creating your first branch is the very first step.
tour.branchCreateBody: Click the «Add New Branch» button to open the drawer. Inside, fill in the branch name and address, then hit Save. Once your branch is saved you'll be ready to add employees to it.
tour.employeePageBody: Now that you have a branch, head to the Employees page. Employees are the people your system tracks for attendance, tasks, and AI detections — and each one must be assigned to a branch you already created.
tour.employeeCreateBody: Click «Add New Employee» to open the drawer. Fill in the employee's details and assign them to your branch. Once saved, this employee can be placed inside a department.
tour.deptPageBody: Departments are teams or divisions inside a branch — for example Kitchen, Security, or Front-of-House. Now that you have a branch and an employee, you have everything you need to create your first department.
tour.deptCreateBody: Click «Add New Department» to open the drawer. Name the department, assign it to your branch, and save. With Branch ✓, Employee ✓, and Department ✓ in place, your workspace is fully set up and you can use the entire system without any issues. Enjoy! 🎉
tour.cameraPageBody: Connect and configure your AI cameras.
tour.dashboardBody: Your operations at a glance. You're all set!
tour.tourCompleteBody: You're ready to start using Rgeeb. Enjoy! 🚀

rules.title: AI Task Generation Rules
rules.subtitle: Configure rules for automated task generation from AI detections
rules.howDesc: When an AI worker sends a detection event, the system checks if a rule exists for that service. If enabled, a task is automatically created with the configured priority, SLA deadline, and assignee. Deduplication prevents duplicate tasks within the configured time window.
